data.description:
## Why

Audit Reliability finding #2 (HIGH). The push-retry path (_store/push.py:159-228) performs the same fetch + git merge + stash mutation that _store/sync.py:130-175 correctly guards with the write lock — but unlocked, by documented design (push_after_commit runs after the lock is released). A concurrent writer that checked the rebase guard before the merge began can have its git commit fail against the transient MERGE_HEAD/index activity, spuriously failing the user's write. In REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=async mode this race is routine: a detached background process merges while the foreground batch keeps writing. Related asyncpush observability gap (audit 3.2): the detached spawn swallows its own OSError and writes to DEVNULL.

## What

Take the write lock (short timeout, attempts=1 like sync.py's read path) around the merge portion of the push retry only — the network push itself stays lockless. Log async-push spawn failures instead of passing silently; optionally have the child write a status line to a gitignored file that fsck can surface alongside PUSH_PENDING. Add a regression test interleaving a push-retry merge with a concurrent locked write (audit gap 7.3).

## Scope

In: src/rebar/_store/push.py (lock acquisition around merge, spawn-failure logging), a concurrency test. Out: sync.py (already correct), changing push best-effort semantics (a push failure must still never fail the write), stash-drop hardening (separate low finding).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The non-fast-forward reconcile merge in push.py runs under the store write lock; lock-timeout means the merge is skipped (push stays pending) rather than racing
- [ ] Async-push spawn failure is logged at warning level (no silent pass)
- [ ] New test: concurrent locked write during a push-retry merge — the write succeeds (no spurious StoreError) and no event is lost
- [ ] Push remains best-effort: no code path lets a push or merge failure propagate to the caller of the write
- [ ] Existing push/sync suites and tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py green
data.title: Take the write lock around the push-retry reconcile merge
